subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Primordial Element Abundances
description: Learn Primordial Element Abundances in one minute.

hook.text: The Big Bang predicted exact ratios: 75% hydrogen, 25% helium, traces of lithium. Measurements match perfectly.

buildup.text: Stars create heavier elements, but the lightest ones trace back to the universe's first three minutes.

discovery.text: Ralph Alpher and George Gamow calculated these abundances in 1948—decades before they could be precisely measured.

twist.text: There's a lithium problem: the Big Bang predicts three times more lithium-7 than we observe. Nobody knows why.

climax.text: Despite the lithium puzzle, the hydrogen-helium ratio is one of the strongest confirmations of Big Bang cosmology.

punchline.text: The recipe from 13.8 billion years ago. We can still read the ingredients.

quiz.question: What is the 'lithium problem' in Big Bang cosmology?
options: The predicted amount of lithium-7 is three times more than what we observe
Lithium should not exist at all according to Big Bang theory
Too much lithium was found in the oldest stars
